---
title: "Noise analysis"
subtitle: "T5 · Phase T5.2 · `va-acnoise::noise`"
---

::: {.callout-note}
**Status: honestly unimplemented — a stretch goal, per `CLAUDE.md` §1, that stayed one, and
blocked on `t5-acnoise/01-ac.qmd` besides.** Same real, compiling stub shape as AC linearization;
`run` itself is `todo!()`.
:::

## Goal

Propagate every device's own noise source through the linearized (AC) circuit to one
output-referred power spectral density, via the adjoint method.

## Where it fits

```
 ac::AcSweep + an output unknown ──► [noise::run] ──► NoiseSpectrum{f, psd, total}
```

This phase depends on T5.1 existing first — noise propagation needs the same frequency-swept
linearization AC analysis would build, there's no separate small-signal machinery to construct
twice.

## The idea (as designed, not yet built)

The adjoint method computes one output's sensitivity to *every* noise source in a single solve
per frequency, rather than one solve per source — the standard reason SPICE-family simulators
use it instead of a naive source-by-source sweep. `docs/validation.md`'s own stated first target
is the simplest possible case to validate against: a single resistor's Johnson-Nyquist thermal
noise, `4kTR` (V²/Hz), a closed-form answer with no reference simulator needed to check the very
first result against.

## The code

```rust
pub struct NoiseSpectrum {
    pub f: Vec<f64>,
    pub psd: Vec<f64>,     // V²/Hz or A²/Hz
    pub total: f64,        // integrated over the swept band
}

pub fn run(_x_dc: &[f64], _sweep: AcSweep, _output: usize) -> Result<NoiseSpectrum, AcNoiseError> {
    todo!("T5: adjoint noise propagation to the output PSD")
}
```

## It works

It doesn't, yet:

```console
$ cargo test -p va-acnoise noise::
test noise::tests::resistor_thermal_noise ... ignored, T5: resistor thermal-noise PSD matches 4kTR vs golden
```

## Limitations

Everything, same as `t5-acnoise/01-ac.qmd` — no partial capability exists to caveat around.

## What's next

Nothing downstream in this crate depends on it; T5 is the end of its own chain. See
`docs/roadmap.md`'s T5 section for the fallback deliverable if no student ever picks this up.
